text.title: Spec Requirements Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Spec Requirements. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every requirement is numbered FR-NNN or NFR-NNN; numbers are stable across revisions.
rule.rationale: Stable numbers let downstream docs reference FR-7 forever.

rule.statement: Each requirement is one declarative sentence; multi-sentence statements split into multiple FRs.
rule.rationale: Multi-sentence requirements bundle multiple checks; splitting forces atomic verification.

rule.statement: Every requirement carries a verification method (unit test, integration test, manual check, monitor).
rule.rationale: Without verification, requirements cannot be regression-checked.

rule.statement: Every requirement carries priority: must / should / could; deferred is not a priority.
rule.rationale: Without priority, scope cuts are arbitrary.

rule.statement: Forbidden tokens: fast, easy, simple, robust, scalable; reject with rewrite.
Each FR/NFR appears in impl-plan and test-plan; orphan requirements flagged.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-spec-requirements.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
